memoryArtifacts.headline: Rock House, Kinver
memoryArtifacts.description:
The Rock House in Kinver where people lived in caves until the modern era.
This has been a landmark in Kinver and tourist attraction there since Victorian times.

sourceList.title: An account of the Offley family

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Leveson於1457年5月7日出生在英國斯塔福爾金巴時，他的父親Nicholas Leveson72歲，母親Maud DePrestwood48歲。他於1500年與Hilary Willenhall在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1503年10月7日在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾沃爾維哈普頓享年46歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Leveson于1457年5月7日出生在英国斯塔福爾金巴时，他的父亲Nicholas Leveson72岁，母亲Maud DePrestwood48岁。他于1500年与Hilary Willenhall在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1503年10月7日在英国英格兰斯塔福爾沃爾維哈普頓享年46岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾。

nameMeanings.name: Leveson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Leveson, Loveson (Old English Lēofsunu).

variants.name: Levison
meanings: English: variant of Leveson, from the Middle English personal name Leveson, Loveson (Old English Lēofsunu, from lēof ‘dear, beloved’ + sunu ‘son’).

variants.name: Lewison
meanings: Welsh and English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Lewis   (in Wales this was taken as equivalent to the Welsh personal name   Llewelyn  ). In North America, this surname has reportedly absorbed in some cases the Greek patronymic   Iliopoulos  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name Lewi, Polish-influenced form of Levy .

variants.name: Leverson
meanings: Altered form of Jewish   Levison   or   Levinson  .

variants.name: Lowson
meanings: 1 English, Scottish: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Low, a pet form of Laurence ( see Lawrence ) + -son. Compare Lawson , Larson , and Lorrison .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Leveson was born on 7 May 1457, in Kinver, Staffordshire, England, his father, Nicholas Leveson, was 72 and his mother, Maud DePrestwood, was 48. He married Hilary Willenhall in 1500, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 7 October 1503, in Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Leveson est né le 7 mai 1457, à Kinver, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Leveson, avait 72 ans et sa mère, Maud DePrestwood, avait 48 ans. Il a épousé Hilary Willenhall en 1500, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 7 octobre 1503, à Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Leveson am 7. Mai 1457 in Kinver, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nicholas Leveson 72 und seine Mutter Maud DePrestwood 48. Er hat 1500 in England, United Kingdom Hilary Willenhall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 7. Oktober 1503 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Leveson è nato il il 7 maggio 1457 nel Kinver, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Nicholas Leveson, aveva 72 anni e il suo madre, Maud DePrestwood, aveva 48 anni. Lui sposato Hilary Willenhall nel 1500, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 7 ottobre 1503, nel Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Levesonが1457年5月7日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアキンバーで生まれた時，父Nicholas Levesonは72歳，母Maud DePrestwoodは48歳であった。1500年にイギリスイギリスでHilary Willenhallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1503年10月7日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアウオルバーハンプトンで46歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Leveson(이)가 1457년 5월 7일 영국 스태퍼드실르 킨버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas Leveson(은)는 72세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud DePrestwood(은)는 48세였다. 그는 1500년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Hilary Willenhall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1503년 10월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 울버함프턴에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르에 묻혔다.

pt.7 May 1457–7 October 1503: 7 de maio de 1457–7 de outubro de 1503
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Leveson nasceu em 7 de maio de 1457, em Kinver, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Leveson, tinha 72 anos e sua mãe, Maud DePrestwood, tinha 48 anos. Ele casou-se com Hilary Willenhall em 1500, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de outubro de 1503, em Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Leveson родился 7 мая 1457 г. в местности кинвир, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Nicholas Leveson было 72 года, а матери, которую звали Maud DePrestwood, 48. Он женился на женщине по имени Hilary Willenhall в 1500 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 7 октября 1503 г. в местности волвирамптон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности шропшир, Англия.

es.7 May 1457–7 October 1503: 7 de mayo de 1457–7 de octubre de 1503
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Leveson nació el 7 de mayo de 1457, en Kinver, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Leveson, tenía 72 años y su mamá, Maud DePrestwood, tenía 48 años. Se casó con Hilary Willenhall en 1500, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 7 de octubre de 1503, en Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Shropshire, Inglaterra.
